title: geum aleppicum var. bipinnatum
intro: Geum aleppicum var. bipinnatum, also known as the yellow avens or bipinnate avens, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Geum aleppicum var. bipinnatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying and processing the roots and leaves, and can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
